content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence of distinct non-negative integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.

For every non-negative integer x<2Kx \lt 2^Kx<2K, let's define a function f(x)f(x)f(x) as the number of inversions in the sequence A1⊕x,A2⊕x,…,AN⊕xA_1 \oplus x, A_2 \oplus x, \dots, A_N \oplus xA1​⊕x,A2​⊕x,…,AN​⊕x. (An inversion in a sequence X1,X2,…,XNX_1, X_2, \dots, X_NX1​,X2​,…,XN​ is a pair of indices (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that i<ji \lt ji<j and Xi>XjX_i \gt X_jXi​>Xj​.)

Consider the sorted sequence of pairs (f(x),x)(f(x), x)(f(x),x) for all integers x∈[0,2K−1]x \in [0, 2^K - 1]x∈[0,2K−1]; a pair (f(x1),x1)(f(x_1), x_1)(f(x1​),x1​) is earlier than (f(x2),x2)(f(x_2), x_2)(f(x2​),x2​) in this sequence if f(x1)<f(x2)f(x_1) \lt f(x_2)f(x1​)<f(x2​), or if f(x1)=f(x2)f(x_1) = f(x_2)f(x1​)=f(x2​) and x1<x2x_1 \lt x_2x1​<x2​. You should find the second element (i.e. the number xxx) of the PPP-th pair in this sequence. The sequence is indexed from 1.
constraints: The elements of A are pairwise distinct
The sum of N for all test cases does not exceed 10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers N, K, and P. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the number x generating the P-th pair in the sorted sequence.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: The values of f(x) for all 0 ≤ x ≤ 7 are as follows: x = 0: f(0) = number of inversions in [2, 0, 3, 7] = 1, x = 1: f(1) = number of inversions in [3, 1, 2, 6] = 2, x = 2: f(2) = number of inversions in [0, 2, 1, 5] = 1, x = 3: f(3) = number of inversions in [1, 3, 0, 4] = 2, x = 4: f(4) = number of inversions in [6, 4, 7, 3] = 4, x = 5: f(5) = number of inversions in [7, 5, 6, 2] = 5, x = 6: f(6) = number of inversions in [4, 6, 5, 1] = 4, x = 7: f(7) = number of inversions in [5, 7, 4, 0] = 5. The sorted sequence of pairs (f(x), x) is [(1, 0), (1, 2), (2, 1), (2, 3), (4, 4), (4, 6), (5, 5), (5, 7)]. We should find the P = 5-th pair, which is (4, 4), so the answer is 4.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to calculate the number of inversions in the sequence A1⊕x, A2⊕x, …, AN⊕x for all x ∈ [0, 2^K - 1]. We can use a brute force approach to calculate the number of inversions for each x and then sort the pairs (f(x), x) to find the P-th pair. The time complexity of this approach is O(T * 2^K * N^2) due to the nested loops for calculating inversions and sorting. The space complexity is O(2^K) for storing the pairs (f(x), x).
